skill.name: exa-research
skill.description: Research current web content and open source code examples with Exa AI. The skill provides workflows, query patterns, and helper scripts for technical research.
skill.summary: Research web sources and code examples with Exa AI workflows.
skill.author: Calel33

security_audit.summary: Static command execution and crypto findings were false positives caused by Markdown code fences, prose, and ordinary dictionary key labels. The skill is legitimate research tooling, but it runs helper scripts and sends user search terms to Exa tools, so publication should include a network and API-key warning.

medium_findings.title: External API Queries May Expose Search Terms
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to call Exa web and code search tools with user-provided research topics. This is legitimate behavior, but confidential project names, proprietary code terms, or sensitive questions may be sent to an external service. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for network exposure. confidence: 0.82. confidence_reasoning: Tool calls are explicit in documentation and scripts, and no malicious destination was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Tool calls are explicit in documentation and scripts. The destination appears to be the documented Exa integration, not an unknown endpoint.

medium_findings.title: Executable Helper Scripts Run Networked Research Workflows
medium_findings.description: The package includes Python scripts that accept command-line topics and pass them into Exa research functions. No shell execution was found, but running these scripts can perform external searches and print derived results. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for script and network risk. confidence: 0.74. confidence_reasoning: The scripts are straightforward and non-malicious, but they automate external API calls from user input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts are present and executable. Their behavior is limited to query preparation and documented Exa calls.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external_commands findings point to Markdown fenced examples and Python snippets, not Ruby backtick execution or shell invocation. No subprocess, os.system, curl, wget, or dynamic shell call was found in the reviewed files. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. confidence: 0.98. confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation blocks or examples, and executable scripts do not contain shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The repeated pattern is Markdown syntax, not executable Ruby or shell code. Python scripts were checked separately and do not spawn commands.

low_findings.title: API Key Documentation Is Not Secret Exfiltration
low_findings.description: README.md documents that users need an Exa API key in a local .env file. The value shown is a placeholder, and no reviewed skill code reads or transmits local secret files directly. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for secret leakage, with expected env_access configuration risk. confidence: 0.9. confidence_reasoning: The evidence is installation documentation only, but users should still treat API keys as sensitive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited key is a placeholder in setup instructions. No code in this skill directly reads .env or prints the key.

low_findings.title: Crypto, Certificate, and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, certificate/key file, and system reconnaissance findings map to ordinary prose, examples, or dictionary key wording. No cryptographic algorithm usage, certificate parsing, private key handling, or host reconnaissance behavior was found at the cited lines. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. confidence: 0.93. confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations contain documentation text or result-key labels, not security-sensitive operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were inspected and do not contain cryptographic operations or system probing. The scanner appears to match broad words such as key or search.

content.user_title: Research web and code examples with Exa
content.value_statement: Technical research often requires current articles and working code examples. This skill combines Exa web search, code context retrieval, and repeatable workflows for faster investigation.
actual_capabilities: Search web articles, news, documentation, and guides through Exa web search.
Retrieve code examples and implementation patterns through Exa code context search.
Recommend query wording for web searches and code searches.
Run scripted workflows for technology, implementation, comparison, and discovery research.
Provide reusable query patterns for common technical research tasks.
Combine web context with code examples for implementation planning.
limitations: Requires a configured Exa API key before search tools can work.
Sends search queries to an external Exa service.
Cannot guarantee that retrieved web content is accurate or current.
Does not access private repositories unless the Exa integration supports them.

use_cases.title: Learn a new framework
use_cases.description: Research framework features, current guidance, and starter code before implementation.

use_cases.title: Compare implementation options
use_cases.description: Gather comparison articles and code examples for competing tools or libraries.

use_cases.title: Prepare technical documentation
use_cases.description: Find examples, common patterns, and recent references for documentation work.

prompt_templates.title: Find Current Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Research {topic} with Exa web search. Return recent sources, key points, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Find Code Examples
prompt_templates.prompt: Find code examples for {framework} {feature}. Summarize common patterns and setup requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare {option_a} and {option_b}. Use web search for context and code search for implementation differences.

prompt_templates.title: Build Research Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a research plan for {technology}. Include overview, ecosystem, implementation examples, risks, and validation sources.

output: A summary of current authentication approaches for FastAPI.
Implementation examples covering JWT, OAuth, and dependency-based authorization.
A short list of follow-up questions for production readiness.
A side-by-side comparison of features, migration support, and developer workflow.
Code example themes for schema definition, querying, and migrations.
A recommendation based on project size and team preferences.
Recent guidance from web sources and documentation.
Common optimization patterns found in code examples.
Tradeoffs for memoization, server rendering, and bundle splitting.

best_practices: Remove confidential project names or secrets from search queries.
Use both web search and code search for implementation decisions.
Validate important findings against official documentation when possible.
anti_patterns: Sending proprietary source code or credentials as search terms.
Using broad one-word queries when a framework or feature is known.
Treating search results as authoritative without source review.

faq.question: What does this skill use Exa for?
faq.answer: It uses Exa for web search and code context retrieval during technical research.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The README documents an Exa API key in a local environment file.

faq.question: Can it find working code examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses Exa code context search to retrieve examples and implementation patterns.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential research?
faq.answer: Use caution. Search terms are sent to an external service and should not include secrets.

faq.question: Does it execute shell commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found. Static shell findings were Markdown code fence false positives.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
